pages.raw_text:
Another complaint against the
Red Cross employment agency is
lodged today by j. Almond, Jack
Davis and Pete Eurn, who claim
they were hired as transfers for
railroad work on Snake river and
were told when the camp was
reached that no teamsters were
needed and had not been for three
weeks past. On the way back they
met four men at the ferry, sent
down by the same agency for the
same kind OF work at the same
place.


The men were given passes
down but had tot pay their way
back. Eurh Vas ont $4.40 For a
ticket and he threw away his
change of clothes and bedding on
rs mile walk in order to make
the return trip.


The agency here paid them back
the $1.75 fee charged, but there
was no reimbursement for the ex.
pense money the experience cost.
They claim to have discovered that
